methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $1.0B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 8 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $120M - $6.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

private.name: KoBold Metals
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series C), post-money $2B+

private.name: Amogy
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series C), post-money $700M

data.narrative:
Electric Hydrogen, a Series C climate and energy company founded by Raffi Garabedian and David Eaglesham, has raised $380M to date against a stated valuation of $1.0B+. The valuation analysis benchmarks against a peer set of eight same-sector, same/adjacent-stage companies including KoBold Metals ($2.0B Series C), Amogy ($700M Series C), and Redwood Materials ($6.0B Series D), alongside one public comparable (LNZA).

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, anchoring to the $1.0B last post-money valuation and applying a typical 1.7x Series C step-up multiplier, implies a valuation of $1.7B. Peer-median analysis across the eight-company climate and energy cohort yields a median valuation of $450M, with a range spanning $120M to $6.0B. The wide peer dispersion reflects heterogeneity in technology maturity and capital intensity across the sector.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $382M (low) to $2.0B (high), with a median of $1.1B. The current $1.0B+ valuation sits near the lower bound of this range. Key uncertainties include the company's specific technology readiness level and path to commercialization relative to peers, both of which drive significant valuation variance within the climate tech sector.
